slides.title: Introduction to Convolutional Neural Networks
slides.subtitle: Exploring the Architecture and Applications of CNNs

slides.title: What are CNNs?
slides.content: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are a class of deep neural networks, most commonly applied to analyzing visual imagery. They are specifically designed to process pixel data and have proven highly effective for tasks such as object recognition and semantic segmentation.

slides.title: CNN Architecture
slides.content: CNNs are made up of layers such as convolutional layers, pooling layers, and fully connected layers. Each type of layer plays a specific role in processing and transforming the input data.

slides.title: Convolution Operation
slides.content: The convolution operation involves applying a filter (or kernel) to the input to produce a feature map. This operation helps CNNs detect spatial hierarchies in data.

slides.title: Performance Metrics
slides.content: CNNs are evaluated based on various performance metrics such as accuracy, precision, and recall. Such metrics help in assessing the model's effectiveness on tasks.

slides.title: Applications of CNNs
slides.content: CNNs are widely used in image and video recognition, recommender systems, image classification, medical image analysis, and natural language processing. Their ability to effectively capture spatial hierarchies in data makes them ideal for such tasks.

slides.title: Example: Image Classification
slides.content: In image classification, CNNs take an image as input, process it across various layers, and output a set of class probabilities.

visual_assets.code:
# Example of image classification using CNN
model = Sequential([
    Conv2D(32, (3, 3), activation='relu', input_shape=(64, 64, 3)),
    MaxPooling2D(pool_size=(2, 2)),
    Flatten(),
    Dense(units=128, activation='relu'),
    Dense(units=10, activation='softmax')
])
